title: 10 Herbal Essential Oils For Urinary Calculus
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils have been used for centuries to help alleviate urinary calculus, also known as kidney stones. These tiny stones can form in the kidneys when minerals in the urine become concentrated. Herbal teas, which are made from these essential oils, can help dissolve and flush out these stones, providing relief from the intense pain and discomfort they can cause.  For example, juniper berries, extracted from the Juniperus communis tree, have antispasmodic and diuretic properties that help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract and increase urine production, making it easier to pass the stones. Licorice root, extracted from the Glycyrrhiza glabra plant, has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the kidneys and urinary tract. Horsetail, extracted from the Equisetum arvense plant, is rich in silica and can help to strengthen the kidneys and urinary tract, making them more efficient at filtering and removing waste products.  Drinking herbal teas made from these essential oils can bring several benefits to your life. For one, it can provide relief from the excruciating pain and discomfort caused by urinary calculus. It can also help to reduce the risk of future stone formation by keeping the urinary tract healthy and functioning properly. Furthermore, these teas can be a natural and gentle way to promote overall health and well-being, making them a great addition to any wellness routine. By incorporating these teas into your daily routine, you can take a proactive approach to maintaining a healthy urinary tract and preventing future problems.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for urinary calculus

remedies.remedy_desc: Juniperus communis essential oils contains a high concentration of sesquiterpenes and monoterpene hydrocarbons. The active constituents, including alpha-pinene and beta-pinene, have a diuretic effect, helping to increase urine production and flush out the urinary system. The oils also contain sabinene, a compound that has been shown to have a mild antibacterial and anti-inflammatory action, which can help prevent infection and reduce inflammation in the urinary tract. The diuretic properties of Juniperus communis essential oils can help prevent the formation of urinary calculi by increasing the flow of urine and reducing the concentration of minerals that can form stones. The increased urine production also helps to dissolve and eliminate existing stones, reducing the risk of discomfort and other complications associated with urinary calculi.
remedies.image_alt: Juniperus communis herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Juniperus communis berries and 2 cups of water.
Combine the berries and water in a saucepan, bring to a boil, then reduce heat.
Simmer for 30 minutes, then strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like jojoba oil) to the strained mixture and mix well.
Store the Juniperus communis essential oil in a dark glass bottle in the fridge for up to 2 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils contains compounds like glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and saponins that help with urinary calculus. Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. Flavonoids, particularly quercetin, have antioxidant properties that prevent damage to kidney cells. Saponins in Glycyrrhiza glabra essential oils increase urine production, helping to flush out small stones and prevent their formation. This helps to prevent the buildup of urinary calculus and promotes smooth urination.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra roots. Clean and dry them.
Fill a glass jar with 2 cups of the dried roots. Pour in 4 cups of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil) over the roots.
Leave the jar in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks. Shake the jar every day to help the oil soak into the roots.
Strain the oil mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another jar. Discard the roots.
Filter the oil mixture again through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean jar. Store the oil in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Equisetum arvense essential oils contains silicic acid, flavonoids, and phenolic compounds as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain associated with urinary calculus. Silicic acid in Equisetum arvense essential oils also has a diuretic effect, increasing urine production and helping to flush out kidney stones. The flavonoids and phenolic compounds in the oil have antioxidant properties, protecting the kidneys from damage caused by oxidative stress. By reducing inflammation and promoting urine production, Equisetum arvense essential oils may aid in the elimination of urinary calculus.
remedies.image_alt: Equisetum arvense herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 500 grams of Equisetum arvense roots and flowers. Clean and dry them completely.
Use a cup of carrier oil (like jojoba or sweet almond oil) and mix with 25 grams of dried Equisetum arvense. Store in a dark glass bottle.
Leave the mixture for 2-3 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking the bottle every 2 days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another dark glass bottle. Discard the solids.
Store the essential oil in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops in a warm bath for relief from urinary calculus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum essential oils contains boiactive constituents like flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and alkaloids. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the urinary tract from damage. The sesquiterpenes in Silybum marianum essential oils have been shown to have diuretic effects, which can increase urine production and help flush out small stones. The flavonoids in the oil have been found to have antimicrobial properties, reducing the risk of infection in the urinary tract. By reducing inflammation and promoting urine production, Silybum marianum essential oils may help prevent the formation of urinary calculi.
remedies.image_alt: Silybum marianum herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. Chop them into small pieces.
Use a clean glass jar to mix 1 cup of chopped Silybum marianum with 2 cups of carrier oil (such as coconut or olive oil).
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into another clean glass jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Silybum marianum essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use 5-7 drops in a warm bath for relief from urinary calculus.

remedies.remedy_desc: Berberis vulgaris essential oils contains berberine, a bioactive constituent that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Berberine helps to reduce inflammation in the urinary tract and prevent the formation of stones by inhibiting the growth of bacteria that can contribute to the development of urinary calculus. The essential oil also contains alkaloids like berbamine and oxyberberine, which have been found to have a diuretic effect, helping to increase urine production and flush out stones. The antispasmodic properties of Berberis vulgaris essential oils can also help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract and prevent spasms that can contribute to the formation of stones. By combining these properties, Berberis vulgaris essential oils may help to prevent the formation of urinary calculus and alleviate symptoms associated with the condition.
remedies.image_alt: Berberis vulgaris herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of Berberis vulgaris berries and 1 cup of carrier oil (like jojoba oil).
Mash the berries using a mortar and pestle to release their oils. Set aside 1/4 cup of mashed berries.
Combine the mashed berries with the carrier oil in a 1:5 ratio (1 part berries to 5 parts oil). Stir well.
Transfer the mixture to a clean glass jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container. Discard the solids and use the liquid as your essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Urtica dioica essential oils contains the bioactive constituents like histamine, 5-hydroxytryptamine, and urticine. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the urinary tract. The essential oil also contains flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that help protect the kidneys from damage caused by free radicals. Urtica dioica essential oils has diuretic properties, which increase urine production and help flush out small stones from the urinary tract. The essential oil's ability to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria also helps prevent infections that can lead to the formation of kidney stones.
remedies.image_alt: Urtica dioica herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Urtica dioica leaves, flowers, and stems. Clean them with water.
Use a juicer or blender to extract the liquid from the plant material. Get 2 cups of liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a saucepan. Add 1 cup of water and 1/2 cup of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil).
Heat the mixture over low heat for 2-3 hours. Stir occasionally. The liquid will reduce and become thick.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container. Discard the solids. Your Urtica dioica essential oil is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains compounds like linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. These properties help to reduce inflammation and fight off bacterial infections that can contribute to urinary calculus. The essential oil also contains flavonoids like apigenin and luteolin, which have antioxidant properties that help to prevent oxidative stress and damage to the urinary tract. Additionally, the oil's antispasmodic properties can help to relax the muscles in the urinary tract, reducing spasms and cramping that can be associated with urinary calculus. By reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and relaxing muscles, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils may provide relief for individuals experiencing urinary calculus.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of water.
Combine the flowers and water in a saucepan, and heat on low until the mixture boils.
Reduce heat to medium-low and let the mixture simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean bowl, and discard the solids.
Use a steam distillation apparatus or a simple distillation setup to extract the essential oil from the plant material.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hydrangea arborescens essential oils contains a compound called anthocyanin and phenolic acids, which are known to have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce inflammation in the urinary tract, making it a potential remedy for urinary calculus. The oil also contains flavonoids and saponins, which have been shown to have diuretic and antispasmodic effects, helping to flush out the bladder and reduce pain. The diuretic properties of the oil help increase urine production, which can help break down and pass small stones in the urinary tract. By reducing inflammation and promoting urine production, Hydrangea arborescens essential oils may help alleviate symptoms of urinary calculus.
remedies.image_alt: Hydrangea arborescens herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Hydrangea arborescens flowers and 2 cups of water.
Combine the flowers and water in a saucepan and heat it over low heat.
Simmer for 2-3 hours or until the liquid has reduced to 1 cup.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.
Discard the solids and let the liquid cool. Once cooled, transfer it to a dark glass bottle and store in a cool, dark place.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains carnosic acid, bornyl acetate, and camphor as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body. The antioxidant properties of bornyl acetate also help prevent the formation of kidney stones by reducing the concentration of oxalate in the urine. Additionally, camphor has a diuretic effect, which increases urine production and helps flush out toxins and minerals that can contribute to the formation of stones. By reducing inflammation, preventing stone formation, and promoting urine production, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils may help alleviate urinary calculus symptoms.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and flowers. Wear protective gloves and work in a well-ventilated area.
Combine the leaves and flowers in a clean glass jar. Add 2 cups of water and let it steep for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another container. Discard the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a steam distillation apparatus. Distill the mixture for 2-3 hours to extract the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle. Label and date the bottle. Use 5-7 drops in a warm bath or mix with a carrier oil for topical use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melissa officinalis essential oils contains rosmarinic acid, melitoside, and limonene, which are the main bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties that help to reduce inflammation and prevent the formation of urinary calculus. Rosmarinic acid also has a diuretic effect, increasing urine production and helping to flush out the kidneys and bladder. The antioxidant properties of limonene help to reduce oxidative stress and prevent the damage to the kidney and bladder tissues caused by the formation of urinary calculus. By reducing inflammation and promoting the health of the kidneys and bladder, Melissa officinalis essential oils may help to prevent the formation of urinary calculus.
remedies.image_alt: Melissa officinalis herbal essential oils for urinary calculus
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Melissa officinalis flowers and 1 cup of water. Sterilize a glass jar and utensils with soap and hot water.
Combine the Melissa officinalis flowers and water in the sterilized jar. Cover the jar with a lid.
Steep the mixture in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar daily. This will help release the essential oils.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another sterilized jar. Discard the solids.
Store the Melissa officinalis essential oil in a dark, cool place. Use 5-7 drops in a warm bath or 1-2 drops in a diffuser to help with urinary calculus.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent urinary calculus from forming. Some herbal teas, like those with dandelion or juniper berries, can help dissolve small kidney stones and prevent new ones from forming. These teas are thought to work by increasing urine production and reducing acidity, which can help flush out minerals that form stones.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for urinary calculus every day can be safe if you choose the right ones. Look for teas made from herbs like juniper, uva ursi, and corn silk, which are known to help with kidney stones. However, be cautious with teas that contain high amounts of caffeine or tannins, as they can irritate the bladder and kidneys.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with urinary calculus, but it's hard to predict exactly when you'll start feeling better. Some people notice improvements within a few days to a week, while others may take longer, up to 2-3 weeks. Everyone's body is different, and it's also dependent on the type of herbal tea and your individual health.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea for urinary calculus can be best in the morning or early afternoon. This allows the body to process and flush out the stones naturally throughout the day. Drinking at night might not be as effective, as the stones may be reabsorbed while you sleep.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for urinary calculus. For example, some teas like uva ursi and juniper berries may increase the risk of kidney stone formation or worsen existing kidney problems when taken with certain medications. It's essential to be aware of these potential interactions and discuss them with your doctor.
faq_pregnant: It's not recommended to consume herbal teas for urinary calculus while pregnant. Some herbal teas can cause the body to lose too much fluid, which can lead to dehydration and low amniotic fluid levels, a serious issue for the baby's health.
faq_breastfeeding: When breastfeeding, it's best to avoid herbal teas that might affect kidney stone formation or urine flow. Some herbal teas, like those containing juniper or uva ursi, could interfere with urine production or stone formation. To be safe, choose teas that are known to be gentle on the kidneys and urinary system.
title_2: 10 Herbal Remedies for Older Adults with Urinary Calculus Using Essential Oils
